source: Scholarly consensus (Brass, Butalia, Singh) / Oxford History
note: Estimates vary widely; 10–20 million displaced in largest mass migration in history. Pakistani and Indian narratives differ sharply on attribution of violence.

source: British military records / Oxford History of Modern India
note: Combined military casualties on both sides. Tribal lashkar casualties not fully recorded. UN-mandated ceasefire Jan 1949.
Pakistan officially claimed 2,862 dead; India reports similar losses. Both sides claimed victory; Tashkent Declaration signed Jan 1966.

source: Hamoodur Rahman Commission (Pakistan) / Bangladesh government / ICIJ
note: Pakistan's own Hamoodur Rahman Commission acknowledged 26,000+ killed; Bangladesh officially claims 3 million. Pakistani military surrendered 93,000 troops to India Dec 16, 1971.

source: South Asia Terrorism Portal / PIPS Annual Report 2023
note: Includes security forces, civilians, and militants. Peak violence 2007–2014; resurgence after 2021 Taliban takeover of Afghanistan. Over 2,000 attacks in 2023 alone.
TTP attacked Army Public School Peshawar; 132 children among the dead. Deadliest terrorist attack in Pakistan's history. Led to National Action Plan.
Pakistan officially claimed 357 killed but never acknowledged regular army involvement. India put its own losses at 527. Total estimates significantly higher.
Includes army, Baloch militants, and civilians. Pakistan denies enforced disappearances; HRCP documented thousands of missing persons in Balochistan.

source: Government of Pakistan / UNOCHA / World Bank
note: 7.6 magnitude earthquake struck AJK and NWFP. Over 3 million left homeless. Largest natural disaster in Pakistan's recorded history.
Following Imran Khan's arrest, PTI supporters attacked military installations including Corps Commander House Lahore and GHQ Rawalpindi. Government arrested thousands; PTI alleges army provocation.
